confidence_meta.reason: H.4.1 (WALCL/TGA/Reserves) release lands in 2 days (Jul 23) - within the 48h catalyst window. MOVE below 80; scores not cleanly split 2-2.

structured_conclusion.macro_trend: Restrictive-but-Stable Plumbing / Oil-Driven Inflation Risk Building / Asia Equities Rebounding
structured_conclusion.likely_rotation: US semis re-accelerating (SOXX, DRAM) alongside a broad Asia-equity rebound (KOSPI, NI225, EWY); Korea gate still closed but the gap is narrowing
structured_conclusion.next_catalyst: H.4.1 release Jul 23 (2 days); FOMC Jul 28-29 (7 days)
structured_conclusion.korea_gate: Closed 1/3 - USDKRW pass only; EWY below SMA20 (173.29 vs 182.88) despite +6.4% rally; KOSPI below 8,200 (6,747.96) despite +3.6% rally

bias: Plumbing has been unchanged for a third straight session (still the Jul 15 H.4.1 print, next release Jul 23) while oil keeps grinding higher (+2.8% today, +13% since Jul 8) and breakevens are starting to tick up in sympathy - a modest but real shift from the 'calm breakevens' framing that held for two weeks. Meanwhile yesterday's sharp Asia-equity selloff (Korea, Japan) fully reversed today (KOSPI +3.6%, NI225 +3.3%, EWY +6.4%), suggesting that move was idiosyncratic rather than a funding-stress signal.

counterargs.thesis: Plumbing stable / risk-on continuation into the Jul 23 H.4.1 print
supporting: WALCL/TGA/reserves unchanged for a 3rd session - still Jul 15 vintage, no fresh drain
VIX -4.3% further to 17.11 - equity vol continuing to calm
Korea/Japan equities fully reversed yesterday's selloff (KOSPI +3.6%, NI225 +3.3%, EWY +6.4%, Samsung +6.2%, SK Hynix +4.1%)
SOFR eased 2bps to 3.57%, no funding stress
against: Oil +2.8% today, now +13% since Jul 8 on the Iran conflict/Hormuz risk - breakevens starting to react
US10Y/30Y rose for a 2nd straight session, curve still restrictive into the Jul 28-29 FOMC
RRP buffer still at effective zero - no cushion left ahead of H.4.1
USDJPY pushed further above the 162 BOJ threshold to 163.01
ALLY/CACC/COF credit proxies all softer today despite tighter HY OAS
counterargs.read: Today reads as a partial unwind of yesterday's Asia-equity stress rather than a change in the underlying liquidity picture - the Fed's balance sheet hasn't moved in three sessions and won't until the Jul 23 H.4.1 print, now the key near-term catalyst. The more persistent signal is oil, which keeps climbing on an unresolved geopolitical conflict and is finally showing up - modestly - in breakevens. That's the thread to watch: if T5YIE/T10YIE keep rising alongside oil into next week, the 'calm breakevens' framing starts to break down.

trade_table.rationale: SOXX +4.5%, DRAM +9.3% today; SOXX/SPY ratio back up to 0.741 from 0.712 yesterday - reclaiming relative strength.
Gate still closed 1/3, but today's rebound (KOSPI +3.6%, EWY +6.4%, Samsung +6.2%, SK Hynix +4.1%) fully reverses yesterday's selloff - watch for EWY to reclaim its 20-day SMA (182.88).
2nd straight session of the curve backing up into the Jul 28-29 FOMC; TLT -0.30% today.
+13% since Jul 8 on Iran conflict/Hormuz risk; breakevens starting to tick up in sympathy for the first time this cycle.

catalysts.note: First plumbing move since the Jul 15 vintage - within 48h

catalysts.event: US-Iran conflict / Strait of Hormuz shipping risk
catalysts.note: Primary driver of oil's rise; breakevens beginning to react
Key inflation print given oil's rise

plumbing.tga_delta: flat vs Jul 20 - still Jul 15 H.4.1 vintage, next release Jul 23 (2 days)
plumbing.reserves_delta: flat vs Jul 20 - same H.4.1 vintage

duration.move_delta: +2.51% vs Jul 20 stale print (70.88->72.66), first fresh data since Friday

duration_score.classification: Real-Rate Repricing / Bear Steepening (2nd session), Oil Risk Building
duration_score.reason: US10Y/30Y rose for a second straight session while nominal US02Y stayed sticky (4.257%, +4bps vs Jul 20). T5YIE/T10YIE breakevens ticked up modestly (+3-4bps) for the first time this cycle, alongside oil's continued climb - an early sign the 'calm breakevens' read is starting to shift. DFII5 (2.01%, flat) and DFII10 (2.31%, flat) show the TIPS market itself isn't yet stressed. Kept only mildly negative given plumbing is unchanged (not actively tightening) and VIX continues to calm.

james_framework.what_market_IS_saying: Fed staying restrictive for longer into the FOMC; front-end sticky, long-end drifting higher on term premium - not yet a fresh inflation shock, but oil is testing that read.
james_framework.pain_trade: Real-rate pressure on long duration (TLT -0.30% today, -1.1% over 2 sessions), not CPI panic - though breakevens are starting to move.
james_framework.watch_signal: If T5YIE/T10YIE keep rising in tandem with oil next week, that confirms a shift from real-rate/term-premium repricing toward genuine inflation reacceleration.

breakevens.breakeven_curve_signal: Breakevens (T5YIE/T10YIE) ticked up modestly for the first time this cycle alongside oil's continued rise - still calm in absolute terms but worth watching for confirmation of an inflation-reacceleration regime.
